descripcion: Satin weave fabric offering an extremely soft, smooth, and shiny surface. It is the preferred choice for making evening gowns, high-end lingerie, and decorative elements seeking a touch of luxury and distinction.

Lightweight, fresh, and highly breathable fabric with a composition optimized for rest. Its softness against the skin makes it perfect for creating custom sheet sets, pillowcases, and durable bedding.

Fabric with great body and mechanical resistance, made with tightly interlaced threads. Its versatility and smooth finish make it the ideal material for furniture upholstery, making cushions, backpacks, and craft projects requiring durability.

A resistant variant of canvas featuring graphic designs and motifs via high-quality printing. It combines the robustness needed to upholster chairs and sofas with high decorative value for curtains, tablecloths, and home accessories.

A selection of high-quality cotton fabrics with various coordinated designs. They provide the necessary weight and stability for precise cutting in creative sewing projects, hand quilting, and joining scraps for unique blankets and accessories.

Interlocked textile structure providing natural elasticity and great recovery capacity. It is an extremely comfortable fabric that adapts to the body, ideal for making t-shirts, fitted dresses, sportswear, and children's clothing.

Loop-pile fabric with high absorption capacity thanks to its cotton loop structure. Its fluffy touch and resistance to frequent washing make it essential for manufacturing bath towels, bathrobes, and hygiene accessories.

Specialized fabric for table service that combines stain resistance with an elegant drape. It is designed to withstand daily use and intensive washing while maintaining its color and texture in dining and hospitality environments.

Technical reinforcement material applied to the inside of garments to provide structure, body, and controlled rigidity. Essential for shaping collars, cuffs, lapels, and facings, ensuring the garment maintains its original design.

Fine, dense weave fabric with a slightly silky touch. It is a very rewarding material for sewing due to its stability, being the standard for making dress shirts, feminine blouses, and fresh, elegant summer dresses.

Sumptuous fabric with a short, dense pile that creates an unmistakable velvety texture. It reflects light uniquely, providing color depth and elegance in evening suits, jackets, and sophisticated decor elements.

Textile material imitating animal fur with soft synthetic fibers of various lengths. It provides great thermal insulation and a voluminous look, ideal for creating winter coats, creative costumes, and decorative blankets.

Innovative synthetic alternative to leather offering an embossed surface with high resistance and easy maintenance. Its waterproof and durable finish is perfect for upholstering outdoor seating, sofas, and making handbags and ethical fashion accessories.

High-density technical canvas with specific treatments for UV and weather resistance. Its reinforced structure prevents tearing and fading, being the standard material for sun protection on terraces, balconies, and pergolas.

A dynamic collection of textiles following the latest catwalk trends. Includes fiber blends and novel finishes specifically designed for making seasonal garments that require a modern and avant-garde look.

Natural vegetable fiber recognized for its exceptional freshness and antibacterial properties. Its characteristic irregular texture and ability to absorb moisture make it the indispensable luxury fabric for warm climates and summer fashion.

Universal natural fabric valued for its softness, breathability, and hypoallergenic properties. It is the base of the textile industry due to its versatility, allowing for everything from basic daily clothing to high-durability household items.

Technical materials subjected to flame-retardant chemical processes that prevent the spread of fire. They comply with strict safety regulations, being mandatory for curtains and upholstery in public venues, hotels, theaters, and high-risk environments.

A range of high-performance fabrics with very high abrasion resistance ratings. They are designed to withstand constant friction and weight, making them ideal for renewing chairs, armchairs, sofas, and headboards with longevity guarantees.

Delicate and ornamental fabric featuring openwork and floral motifs made with fine threads. It brings a romantic and sophisticated air when used as an overlay on bridal gowns, lingerie details, or decorative finishes on gala garments.

Non-woven textile produced by compacting fibers, resulting in a dense material that does not fray when cut. Its rigidity and warmth make it perfect for school crafts, Christmas decorations, millinery, and industrial applications.

Thermoplastic polymer material with a closed-cell structure, very light and flexible. Its ease of being cut, glued, and heat-molded makes it the star material for costume design, cosplay accessories, and children's crafts.

Very fine and light textile mesh that creates an effect of transparency and volume without adding weight. It is a fundamental fabric in creating veils, ballerina skirts (tutus), volume layers in party dresses, and event decoration.

Stiff, translucent fabric with a characteristic iridescent sheen. Despite its lightness, it holds its shape very well, allowing for the creation of ruffles, bows, and decorative structures in ceremony dresses and luxury packaging.

Extremely airy and delicate open-weave fabric. Its fluid drape and semi-transparency make it ideal for making scarves, multi-layered dresses, and romantic garments seeking lightness and constant movement.

Cotton or wool fabric subjected to a brushing process to lift the fibers and create a soft, fleecy surface. It is excellent as a thermal insulator, being the preferred material for winter pajamas, thermal sheets, and plaid shirts.

High-tech textile with a high percentage of elastane allowing maximum extension in all directions. Designed to offer total freedom of movement in making dance tights, rhythmic gymnastics wear, and skating outfits.

Natural animal fiber with unmatched thermal properties, capable of maintaining body heat even in wet conditions. Its natural elasticity and wrinkle resistance make it perfect for quality sweaters, jackets, and winter accessories.

Luxury fiber from the hair of the Angora goat, known for its silky luster and great warmth despite its low weight. It is a highly valued material in haute couture for its resistance to felting and exceptional softness to the touch.

Dense wool fabric subjected to finishing processes that close the weave, resulting in a smooth surface very resistant to wind. It is the classic and robust fabric par excellence for manufacturing structured coats and formal outerwear.

Regenerated cellulosic fiber combining the comfort of cotton with the drape and sheen of silk. It is a very fresh and soft fabric that adapts perfectly to movement, being the ideal choice for fluid blouses, wide trousers, and light dresses.

Elastic fabric manufactured in a circular shape that lacks side seams. Its structure makes it ideal for making cuffs on sweatshirts, t-shirt collars, and waistbands, facilitating a clean and professional finish on the garment.

A selection of textiles with different degrees of opacity and drape, designed to dress windows. Includes everything from light sheers that let light through to opaque (blackout) fabrics that guarantee privacy and thermal insulation.

Vibrant textile materials with shiny finishes, sequins, or extravagant textures. They are designed to maximize visual impact in parades and parties, allowing for the creation of spectacular costumes with intense colors and unique textures.

Decorative fabrics incorporating traditional festive motifs and colors such as red, green, and gold. Ideal for making themed table linens, Christmas stockings, table runners, and ornamental details for the home.

Technical combination of an outer fabric joined to a layer of polyurethane foam. It provides an immediate quilted effect and insulation, being widely used inside handbags, electronic device cases, and protection panels.

Reinforced cotton fabric with a characteristic diagonal twill that gives it extreme resistance to wear. It is the iconic material for making jeans, work jackets, and accessories requiring an urban look.

Rustic natural fiber fabric (usually jute or hemp) with an open and very resistant weave. Its organic aesthetic makes it popular for industrial-style upholstery, rustic event decoration, gardening, and traditional packaging.

Synthetic fabric variant with very short and compact fibers offering a touch similar to plush. It is extremely soft and cozy, making it suitable for manufacturing baby blankets, jacket linings, and children's toys.

Thick fabric with a reinforced base designed for floor covering. It offers acoustic and thermal insulation properties, as well as a comfortable step, being ideal for fairs, corporate events, or interior space renovation.
